page: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is a premium-class virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 48 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The CPU allocation is shared, and the system is configured with 1.0 thread per core. Memory capacity is 256.0 GB, providing approximately 5.33 GB of RAM per core. The server includes 500 GB of local SSD storage and one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

In benchmark evaluations, the AMD EPYC 9575F processor demonstrates top-tier performance in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, placing in the top 10%. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the top 25%, indicating strong parallel processing capabilities. Memory bandwidth tests show a distinction between cached and uncached operations: small-block cached read-only bandwidth is strong, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths are average. Cryptographic execution and database operations also rank in the top 25%, supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AES and VAES.

The shared CPU allocation model presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, non-burstable maximum CPU utilization, though the high physical core count mitigates resource contention. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of compute and memory resources on a standard virtualization platform, making it suitable for large-scale deployments without the premium of dedicated hardware. This configuration is suited for database operations, multi-threaded data decompression, static web serving, and lightweight language model inference.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 48 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 500 GB of local SSD storage. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, its CPU architecture delivers top-tier single-core performance and strong multi-core capabilities, ranking in the top 10% and top 25% of benchmarks respectively. While memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks remains average, cached read-only bandwidth is strong. The server lacks GPU acceleration, making it less suitable for heavy LLM prompt processing where it exhibits weaker performance. However, its high resource density and strong database operation benchmarks make it highly efficient for database hosting, multi-threaded decompression, and high-throughput static web serving.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is an x86_64 virtual server with 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 500 GB SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-48xCPU-256GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 48 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 500 GB SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A high-density virtual server combining forty-eight AMD EPYC cores and two hundred fifty-six gigabytes of RAM for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 48 shared vCPUs and 256.0 GB RAM
Bundled 500 GB local SSD storage and one public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities
Strong performance in database operations and static web serving
Average uncached memory bandwidth and weak medium-model LLM prompt processing
Suited for database hosting, multi-threaded decompression, and web serving
